messages.content:
=== MOONSHINER TASK BOUNDARY ===

I run the books for our Tuesday hold'em home game and the arguments are always about SIDE POTS, never about the cards. I want a C# library that does just the chip arithmetic: replay a hand's betting ledger, cut the pots, and pay them out given the hand ranks we read off the table (ranks are inputs — I do NOT want a hand evaluator). Namespace SidePots; xunit suite SidePotsTests.cs is the contract (dotnet test); sample hand scripts in testdata/. Nothing beyond what the csproj pulls.

Table setup: new Table(players, buttonSeat) where players is the clockwise seat list of (name, startingStack). At least two players, unique names, positive stacks, button in range — anything else is ArgumentException.

The ledger (Apply(script)): our hand histories are simple text — '#' comments and blank lines ignored, one action per line. Amounts are TOTAL committed after the action, blinds already folded into the totals. Turn order is whatever the sheet says (we trust the sheet), but chip legality is enforced:
- '{name} raise {n}': set that player's total commitment to n; must be strictly above the current bet (the highest total so far) and at most their stack; reaching the full stack means all-in.
- '{name} call': match the current bet, capped by stack — a short call is an all-in for less.
- '{name} check': only legal if their commitment already equals the current bet (or nothing has been bet).
- '{name} fold': player is out, chips stay committed. One house rule: the player currently holding the strictly highest commitment may not fold.
- '{name} allin': commit the full stack whatever the current bet is.
Acting after folding or after going all-in, raising to at or below the current bet, raising past the stack, checking from behind — all InvalidOperationException. Unknown name -> ArgumentException; unparseable line -> FormatException. Expose Committed(name), IsFolded(name), IsAllIn(name), CurrentBet.

BuildPots() — the pot math, exactly this law: first the REFUND — if one non-folded player's total stands strictly above everyone else's, the excess over the second-highest total goes back to them (that's the uncalled top of their raise, reported in Refunds, at most one entry). Then cut the remaining money into layered pots: at each distinct commitment level of the non-folded players (ascending, zeros ignored), the layer collects that slice from EVERY commitment (folded chips stay in), and its eligible players are the non-folded ones committed to at least that level, listed in seat order. A layer whose only eligible player is the lone caller is still a pot (folded money they simply win). Main pot first, then side pots.

Distribute(ranks) — ranks maps EXACTLY the non-folded players to an integer rank, 1 = best hand, ties share (anything else is ArgumentException). Each pot goes to its best-ranked eligible player(s); on a split, each winner takes amount/winners and the odd chips are handed out one each going clockwise starting from the first winner LEFT OF THE BUTTON (seat button+1, wrapping). Returns a payout for EVERY seated player (refund included, 0 if nothing) — every chip committed must come back out.

The tests replay fixture hands and pin every pot, refund and payout to the chip, so follow the laws to the letter. Don't touch SidePotsTests.cs, SidePots.csproj, testdata/ or .gitignore.


PRIOR ATTEMPT FEEDBACK (address before finishing):
- missing_requirements: Distribute does not reject non-positive rank values. A rank of 0 or below is accepted and can defeat rank 1, contradicting “1 = best hand” and the requirement that invalid rank input throw ArgumentException.
- bugs_and_regressions: The fold check counts folded players when determining whether the actor uniquely holds the highest commitment. For example, after `A raise 80`, `B call`, `A fold`, the implementation also permits `B fold` because folded A remains tied at 80. With a third non-folded player committed at 0, BuildPots produces no pots or refund, and Distribute returns none of the 160 committed chips, violating the chip-conservation requirement.
- requirement unmet: Prevent folding behavior that can strand committed chips and ensure every legal ledger remains distributable.
- requirement unmet: Require ranks to map exactly the non-folded players and use valid ranks where 1 is best.
- requirement unmet: Distribute pots, ties, odd chips, refunds, and zero payouts while returning every committed chip.

### BuildPots details

```csharp
public PotReport BuildPots()
{
    // refund
    var live = players.Where(p => !p.Folded).ToList();
    var refunds = new Dictionary<string,int>();
    // effective commitments for layering
    var effective = players.ToDictionary(p => p.Name, p => p.Committed);
    var top = live.Max(p => p.Committed);
    var leaders = live.Where(p => p.Committed == top).ToList();
    if (leaders.Count == 1)
    {
        var leader = leaders[0];
        var second = players.Where(p => p != leader).Select(p => p.Committed).DefaultIfEmpty(0).Max();
        // "strictly above everyone else's": top > second
        if (top > second)
        {
            refunds[leader.Name] = top - second;
            effective[leader.Name] = second;
        }
    }
    // layers
    var levels = live.Select(p => effective[p.Name]).Where(v => v > 0).Distinct().OrderBy(v => v).ToList();
    var pots = new List<Pot>();
    var prev = 0;
    foreach (var level in levels)
    {
        var amount = players.Sum(p => Math.Min(effective[p.Name], level) - Math.Min(effective[p.Name], prev));
        var eligible = live.Where(p => effective[p.Name] >= level).Select(p => p.Name).ToList();
        pots.Add(new Pot(amount, eligible));
        prev = level;
    }
    return new PotReport(pots, refunds);
}
```

### Distribute details

```csharp
public IReadOnlyDictionary<string,int> Distribute(IReadOnlyDictionary<string,int> ranks)
{
    var live = players.Where(p => !p.Folded).Select(p => p.Name).ToHashSet();
    if (ranks.Count != live.Count || ranks.Keys.Any(k => !live.Contains(k)))
        throw new ArgumentException(...);
    if (ranks.Values.Any(v => v < 1)) throw new ArgumentException(...);
    var report = BuildPots();
    var payout = players.ToDictionary(p => p.Name, p => 0);
    foreach (var (name, amount) in report.Refunds) payout[name] += amount;
    foreach (var pot in report.Pots)
    {
        var best = pot.Eligible.Min(name => ranks[name]);
        var winners = pot.Eligible.Where(name => ranks[name] == best).ToList();
        var share = pot.Amount / winners.Count;
        var odd = pot.Amount % winners.Count;
        foreach (var w in winners) payout[w] += share;
        // odd chips: clockwise from button+1
        var seat = (button + 1) % players.Count;
        for (var given = 0; given < odd; )
        {
            var name = players[seat].Name;
            if (winners.Contains(name)) { payout[name]++; given++; }
            seat = (seat + 1) % players.Count;
        }
    }
    return payout;
}
```

Wait — odd chip distribution: "the odd chips are handed out one each going clockwise starting from the first winner LEFT OF THE BUTTON". So starting at seat button+1, walk clockwise; each winner encountered gets one odd chip until they're gone. Since odd < winners.Count (remainder of division), each winner gets at most one. The loop above works: at most one full cycle needed. But careful: `winners.Contains` on List — fine for small n. Termination: odd ≤ winners.Count so within one cycle all odd chips distributed. Good.

Note the walk must start AT button+1 and check if that seat is a winner (OddChip test: button=1, start seat 2 = Cid, winner → gets chip ✓).

### Chip conservation guarantee
Total payouts = sum(refunds) + sum(pots) = sum of commitments. Refund reduces effective commitment; pots sum = sum of effective commitments (each layer slices fully). Each pot fully distributed (share*count + odd = amount). Refunds added. ✓.

Let me double check layer sum telescoping: sum over layers of amount_i = sum over players of effective_i (since min(eff, Lk) = eff for all players? Lk = max level among NON-FOLDED. A FOLDED player could have effective > Lk? Folded player with commitment higher than all live players? Can that happen? If a folded player had the strictly highest commitment... no wait — the strictly-highest holder can't fold, but a tied-highest can fold. Could a folded player's commitment exceed every live player's? Suppose A raise 100, B call 100, A fold. Now A (folded) 100, B (live) 100. Then B raise... B can raise to 150 if stack allows: B 150 > A 100. Live leader unique → refund: second = max(others incl. folded A=100) → refund 50. Effective B = 100 = A. Levels: 100. Pot: 100+100=200. OK conservation.

But: A raise 100, B call 100, A fold, B... can B have effective above the max level? Max level is among live = B's effective. So Lk = max live effective ≥ every live effective; folded effective could be > Lk only if folded commitment > max live effective. Refund step: if one live player strictly above everyone else → reduced to second-highest. After refund, could folded still exceed all live? Example: A (folded) 100, live B 80, live C 60. Is that reachable? A folded with 100 — when A folded, A must NOT have been strictly highest among non-folded at fold time. At fold time some other non-folded player had ≥ 100. That player is still non-folded with ≥ 100 (commitments never decrease)... unless they folded later. Hmm: A 100, B 100, C 60. A folds (tied, ok). B folds? B is non-folded; others non-folded: C 60 → B 100 > 60 → strictly highest → CANNOT fold. So B stays live at 100 ≥ folded A's 100. In general, after the leader-tie chain, at least one live player has ≥ the max folded commitment. Let me prove: let M = max commitment among folded players. When the last player at level ≥ M folded (call them X, folded at commitment c ≥ M), at that moment another non-folded player Y had commitment ≥ c (else X couldn't fold). Y's commitment only grows. If Y later folds at c' ≥ c, again some other non-folded had ≥ c'. Induction: there's always a live player with commitment ≥ current max-folded... wait, more carefully: whenever a player folds, at least one OTHER non-folded player has commitment ≥ the folder's commitment. That other player might fold later, but at their (higher-or-equal) fold time, yet another non-folded has ≥ theirs. Commitments increase along this chain and players are finite, so the chain ends at a player who never folds → live player with commitment ≥ every folded player's. ✓. So Lk (max live) ≥ max folded, thus min(eff, Lk) = eff for folded too... but wait, that's pre-refund. Refund: if unique live leader exists, their effective is reduced to second-highest overall, which is ≥ folded max? Second-highest overall = max of all others including folded ≥ folded max. So max live effective after refund = max(second-highest, other live) ≥ folded max. ✓. If no unique leader (tie at top among live), no reduction, max live ≥ folded max by the invariant. ✓.

So pots always capture every committed chip: sum(pots) + sum(refunds) = total committed. Conservation guaranteed by construction + the fold rule.
```csharp
namespace SidePots;

/// <summary>
/// Replays the betting ledger of a single hold'em hand, cuts the side pots
/// and pays them out against caller-supplied hand ranks. Chip arithmetic only.
/// </summary>
public sealed class Table
{
    private sealed class Seat
    {
        public Seat(string name, int stack)
        {
            Name = name;
            Stack = stack;
        }

        public string Name { get; }
        public int Stack { get; }
        public int Committed { get; set; }
        public bool Folded { get; set; }
        public bool IsAllIn => Committed == Stack;
    }

    private readonly List<Seat> _seats;
    private readonly Dictionary<string, Seat> _byName;
    private readonly int _button;

    public Table(IEnumerable<(string Name, int Stack)> players, int buttonSeat)
    {
        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(players);
        var list = players.ToList();
        if (list.Count < 2)
            throw new ArgumentException("A table needs at least two players.", nameof(players));
        _seats = new List<Seat>(list.Count);
        _byName = new Dictionary<string, Seat>(StringComparer.Ordinal);
        foreach (var (name, stack) in list)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(name))
                throw new ArgumentException("Player names must be non-empty.", nameof(players));
            if (stack <= 0)
                throw new ArgumentException($"Player '{name}' must start with a positive stack.", nameof(players));
            var seat = new Seat(name, stack);
            if (!_byName.TryAdd(name, seat))
                throw new ArgumentException($"Duplicate player name '{name}'.", nameof(players));
            _seats.Add(seat);
        }
        if (buttonSeat < 0 || buttonSeat >= _seats.Count)
            throw new ArgumentException($"Button seat {buttonSeat} is outside 0..{_seats.Count - 1}.", nameof(buttonSeat));
        _button = buttonSeat;
    }
    ...
}
```

```csharp
    public void Apply(string script)
    {
        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(script);
        foreach (var rawLine in script.Split('\n'))
        {
            var line = rawLine;
            var hash = line.IndexOf('#');
            if (hash >= 0) line = line[..hash];
            line = line.Trim();
            if (line.Length == 0) continue;

            var parts = line.Split(Array.Empty<char>(), StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries);
            var seat = Lookup(parts[0]);
            if (seat.Folded)
                throw new InvalidOperationException($"{seat.Name} has folded and cannot act.");
            if (seat.IsAllIn)
                throw new InvalidOperationException($"{seat.Name} is all-in and cannot act.");

            switch (parts.Length)
            {
                case 2:
                    ApplyWord(seat, parts[1]);
                    break;
                case 3 when parts[1] == "raise":
                    if (!int.TryParse(parts[2], out var amount))
                        throw new FormatException($"Cannot parse raise amount in '{line}'.");
                    Raise(seat, amount);
                    break;
                default:
                    throw new FormatException($"Unparseable action line '{line}'.");
            }
        }
    }
```

ApplyWord:
```csharp
    private void ApplyWord(Seat seat, string action)
    {
        switch (action)
        {
            case "call": Call(seat); break;
            case "check": Check(seat); break;
            case "fold": Fold(seat); break;
            case "allin": seat.Committed = seat.Stack; break;
            default: throw new FormatException($"Unknown action '{action}'.");
        }
    }
```

Raise:
```csharp
    private void Raise(Seat seat, int amount)
    {
        if (amount <= CurrentBet)
            throw new InvalidOperationException($"A raise to {amount} must be strictly above the current bet {CurrentBet}.");
        if (amount > seat.Stack)
            throw new InvalidOperationException($"{seat.Name} cannot raise to {amount} with a stack of {seat.Stack}.");
        seat.Committed = amount;
    }
```

```csharp
        seat.Committed = Math.Min(CurrentBet, seat.Stack);
    }
```

```csharp
    public PotReport BuildPots()
    {
        var effective = _seats.ToDictionary(s => s.Name, s => s.Committed);
        var refunds = new Dictionary<string, int>();

        var live = _seats.Where(s => !s.Folded).ToList();
        var top = live.Max(s => s.Committed);
        var leaders = live.Count(s => s.Committed == top);
        if (leaders == 1)
        {
            var leader = live.First(s => s.Committed == top);
            var second = _seats.Where(s => !ReferenceEquals(s, leader)).Max(s => s.Committed);
            if (top > second)
            {
                refunds[leader.Name] = top - second;
                effective[leader.Name] = second;
            }
        }

        var pots = new List<Pot>();
        var previous = 0;
        foreach (var level in live.Select(s => effective[s.Name]).Where(v => v > 0).Distinct().OrderBy(v => v))
        {
            var amount = _seats.Sum(s => Math.Min(effective[s.Name], level) - Math.Min(effective[s.Name], previous));
            var eligible = live.Where(s => effective[s.Name] >= level).Select(s => s.Name).ToList();
            pots.Add(new Pot(amount, eligible));
            previous = level;
        }

        return new PotReport(pots, refunds);
    }
```

```csharp
    public IReadOnlyDictionary<string, int> Distribute(IReadOnlyDictionary<string, int> ranks)
    {
        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(ranks);
        var liveNames = _seats.Where(s => !s.Folded).Select(s => s.Name).ToHashSet(StringComparer.Ordinal);
        if (ranks.Count != liveNames.Count || ranks.Keys.Any(name => !liveNames.Contains(name)))
            throw new ArgumentException("Ranks must name exactly the non-folded players.", nameof(ranks));
        if (ranks.Values.Any(rank => rank < 1))
            throw new ArgumentException("Ranks must be positive integers; 1 is the best hand.", nameof(ranks));

        var report = BuildPots();
        var payouts = _seats.ToDictionary(s => s.Name, _ => 0);

        foreach (var (name, amount) in report.Refunds)
            payouts[name] += amount;

        foreach (var pot in report.Pots)
        {
            var best = pot.Eligible.Min(name => ranks[name]);
            var winners = pot.Eligible.Where(name => ranks[name] == best).ToList();
            var share = pot.Amount / winners.Count;
            var oddChips = pot.Amount - share * winners.Count;
            foreach (var winner in winners)
                payouts[winner] += share;

            var seatIndex = (_button + 1) % _seats.Count;
            while (oddChips > 0)
            {
                if (winners.Contains(_seats[seatIndex].Name))
                {
                    payouts[_seats[seatIndex].Name]++;
                    oddChips--;
                }
                seatIndex = (seatIndex + 1) % _seats.Count;
            }
        }

        return payouts;
    }
```

```csharp
namespace SidePots;

/// <summary>
/// Replays one hand's betting ledger, cuts the layered pots and pays them out
/// against hand ranks read off the table. Chip arithmetic only: no cards here.
/// </summary>
public sealed class Table
{
    private sealed class Seat
    {
        public Seat(string name, int stack)
        {
            Name = name;
            Stack = stack;
        }

        public string Name { get; }
        public int Stack { get; }
        public int Committed { get; set; }
        public bool Folded { get; set; }
        public bool IsAllIn => Committed == Stack;
    }

    private readonly List<Seat> _seats;
    private readonly Dictionary<string, Seat> _byName;
    private readonly int _button;

    /// <summary>players is the clockwise seat list; buttonSeat indexes into it.</summary>
    public Table(IEnumerable<(string Name, int Stack)> players, int buttonSeat)
    {
        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(players);

        var list = players.ToList();
        if (list.Count < 2)
            throw new ArgumentException("A table needs at least two players.", nameof(players));

        _seats = new List<Seat>(list.Count);
        _byName = new Dictionary<string, Seat>(StringComparer.Ordinal);
        foreach (var (name, stack) in list)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(name))
                throw new ArgumentException("Player names must be non-empty.", nameof(players));
            if (stack <= 0)
                throw new ArgumentException($"Player '{name}' must start with a positive stack.", nameof(players));
            var seat = new Seat(name, stack);
            if (!_byName.TryAdd(name, seat))
                throw new ArgumentException($"Duplicate player name '{name}'.", nameof(players));
            _seats.Add(seat);
        }

        if (buttonSeat < 0 || buttonSeat >= _seats.Count)
            throw new ArgumentException($"Button seat {buttonSeat} is outside the seat range.", nameof(buttonSeat));
        _button = buttonSeat;
    }

    /// <summary>The highest total commitment on the table, folded chips included.</summary>
    public int CurrentBet => _seats.Max(seat => seat.Committed);

    public int Committed(string name) => Lookup(name).Committed;
    public bool IsFolded(string name) => Lookup(name).Folded;
    public bool IsAllIn(string name) => Lookup(name).IsAllIn;

    /// <summary>Replays a hand history: '#' comments and blank lines ignored, one action per line.</summary>
    public void Apply(string script)
    {
        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(script);

        foreach (var rawLine in script.Split('\n'))
        {
            var line = rawLine;
            var hash = line.IndexOf('#');
            if (hash >= 0)
                line = line[..hash];
            line = line.Trim();
            if (line.Length == 0)
                continue;

            var parts = line.Split(Array.Empty<char>(), StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries);
            var seat = Lookup(parts[0]);
            if (seat.Folded)
                throw new InvalidOperationException($"{seat.Name} has folded and cannot act.");
            if (seat.IsAllIn)
                throw new InvalidOperationException($"{seat.Name} is all-in and cannot act.");

            if (parts.Length == 3 && parts[1] == "raise")
            {
                if (!int.TryParse(parts[2], out var amount))
                    throw new FormatException($"Cannot parse raise amount in '{line}'.");
                Raise(seat, amount);
            }
            else if (parts.Length == 2)
            {
                Act(seat, parts[1]);
            }
            else
            {
                throw new FormatException($"Cannot parse action line '{line}'.");
            }
        }
    }

    private void Act(Seat seat, string action)
    {
        switch (action)
        {
            case "call":
                // Match the current bet, capped by the stack; a short call is an all-in for less.
                seat.Committed = Math.Min(CurrentBet, seat.Stack);
                break;
            case "check":
                if (seat.Committed != CurrentBet)
                    throw new InvalidOperationException($"{seat.Name} cannot check from behind the bet of {CurrentBet}.");
                break;
            case "fold":
                Fold(seat);
                break;
            case "allin":
                seat.Committed = seat.Stack;
                break;
            default:
                throw new FormatException($"Unknown action '{action}'.");
        }
    }

    private void Raise(Seat seat, int amount)
    {
        if (amount <= CurrentBet)
            throw new InvalidOperationException($"A raise to {amount} must be strictly above the current bet of {CurrentBet}.");
        if (amount > seat.Stack)
            throw new InvalidOperationException($"{seat.Name} cannot raise to {amount} with a stack of {seat.Stack}.");
        seat.Committed = amount;
    }

    private void Fold(Seat seat)
    {
        // House rule: the lone leader may not fold. Measured against the other
        // non-folded players only, so folded chips can never strand the pot.
        var others = _seats.Where(s => !s.Folded && !ReferenceEquals(s, seat)).ToList();
        if (others.Count == 0 || seat.Committed > others.Max(s => s.Committed))
            throw new InvalidOperationException($"{seat.Name} holds the strictly highest commitment and may not fold.");
        seat.Folded = true;
    }

    /// <summary>Refunds the uncalled top of a raise, then cuts the layered pots.</summary>
    public PotReport BuildPots()
    {
        var live = _seats.Where(s => !s.Folded).ToList();
        var effective = _seats.ToDictionary(s => s.Name, s => s.Committed);
        var refunds = new Dictionary<string, int>();

        // Refund: a non-folded player strictly above everyone else (folded
        // included) takes back the excess over the second-highest total.
        var top = live.Max(s => s.Committed);
        if (live.Count(s => s.Committed == top) == 1)
        {
            var leader = live.First(s => s.Committed == top);
            var second = _seats.Where(s => !ReferenceEquals(s, leader)).Max(s => s.Committed);
            if (top > second)
            {
                refunds[leader.Name] = top - second;
                effective[leader.Name] = second;
            }
        }

        // Layers: at each distinct live commitment level, collect that slice
        // from every commitment; eligible are the live players at or above it.
        var pots = new List<Pot>();
        var previous = 0;
        foreach (var level in live.Select(s => effective[s.Name]).Where(v => v > 0).Distinct().OrderBy(v => v))
        {
            var amount = _seats.Sum(s => Math.Min(effective[s.Name], level) - Math.Min(effective[s.Name], previous));
            var eligible = live.Where(s => effective[s.Name] >= level).Select(s => s.Name).ToList();
            pots.Add(new Pot(amount, eligible));
            previous = level;
        }

        return new PotReport(pots, refunds);
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Pays every pot to its best-ranked eligible player(s). Ties split evenly;
    /// odd chips go one each clockwise from the first winner left of the button.
    /// Every seated player gets an entry, refunds included.
    /// </summary>
    public IReadOnlyDictionary<string, int> Distribute(IReadOnlyDictionary<string, int> ranks)
    {
        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(ranks);

        var liveNames = _seats.Where(s => !s.Folded).Select(s => s.Name).ToHashSet(StringComparer.Ordinal);
        if (ranks.Count != liveNames.Count || ranks.Keys.Any(name => !liveNames.Contains(name)))
            throw new ArgumentException("Ranks must cover exactly the non-folded players.", nameof(ranks));
        if (ranks.Values.Any(rank => rank < 1))
            throw new ArgumentException("Ranks must be positive integers; 1 is the best hand.", nameof(ranks));

        var report = BuildPots();
        var payouts = _seats.ToDictionary(s => s.Name, _ => 0);
        foreach (var (name, amount) in report.Refunds)
            payouts[name] += amount;

        foreach (var pot in report.Pots)
        {
            var best = pot.Eligible.Min(name => ranks[name]);
            var winners = pot.Eligible.Where(name => ranks[name] == best).ToHashSet(StringComparer.Ordinal);
            var share = pot.Amount / winners.Count;
            var oddChips = pot.Amount - share * winners.Count;
            foreach (var winner in winners)
                payouts[winner] += share;

            var seatIndex = (_button + 1) % _seats.Count;
            while (oddChips > 0)
            {
                if (winners.Contains(_seats[seatIndex].Name))
                {
                    payouts[_seats[seatIndex].Name] += 1;
                    oddChips -= 1;
                }
                seatIndex = (seatIndex + 1) % _seats.Count;
            }
        }

        return payouts;
    }

    private Seat Lookup(string name)
    {
        if (name is null || !_byName.TryGetValue(name, out var seat))
            throw new ArgumentException($"Unknown player '{name}'.", nameof(name));
        return seat;
    }
}
```

messages.content:
Done. **28/28 tests pass** with only two new files added — `Table.cs` and `Pot.cs` (untouched: `SidePotsTests.cs`, `SidePots.csproj`, `testdata/`, `.gitignore`).

## What was built

**`Table`** (namespace `SidePots`):
- **Setup** — validates ≥2 players, unique non-empty names, positive stacks, button in range; anything else → `ArgumentException`.
- **`Apply(script)`** — strips `#` comments/blank lines, parses `raise n` / `call` / `check` / `fold` / `allin`. Enforces: raise strictly above `CurrentBet` and ≤ stack, check only when not behind, no acting after fold/all-in (all `InvalidOperationException`); unknown name → `ArgumentException`; garbage lines/amounts → `FormatException`. All-in is derived (`Committed == Stack`), so raise-to-stack, full-stack call, and short call all behave per the law.
- **`BuildPots()`** — refund first (unique live leader strictly above *everyone else, folded included* gets the excess over the second-highest total back), then layers cut at each distinct live commitment level, collecting each slice from *all* commitments with live players at/above the level eligible, in seat order.
- **`Distribute(ranks)`** — validates ranks cover exactly the non-folded players and are all ≥ 1; each pot goes to its best eligible rank, splits share `amount/winners` with odd chips dealt one each clockwise from seat `button+1`; returns every seated player, refunds included.

## Prior feedback addressed (verified with throwaway probes, since deleted)

1. **Non-positive ranks** — `rank < 1` now throws `ArgumentException`, so rank 0 can no longer beat rank 1.
2. **Fold rule counted folded players** — the strictly-highest check now compares only against *non-folded* others. The exact failing script (`A raise 80, B call, A fold`) now rejects `B fold`; the remaining ledger cuts one 160-chip pot to Bob and conserves all chips. The last live player can never fold, so no legal ledger can strand money.
3. **Chip conservation** — because the fold rule keeps a live commitment ≥ every folded one, pots + refunds always equal total committed; probes confirmed conservation in tie-fold, heads-up-refund, and folded-second-highest scenarios.

Proof: `dotnet test` → `Passed! - Failed: 0, Passed: 28, Total: 28`.

messages.content:
=== MOONSHINER TASK BOUNDARY ===

I keep score for a Tuesday bowling league and I'm done doing it on paper — I want a small C# library that scores ten-pin exactly by the book, plus a sheet runner our secretary can point at the roll files we already keep (samples are in testdata/). The xunit suite in TenpinTests.cs is the acceptance contract; dotnet test green means done. Namespace Tenpin, no packages beyond what the csproj already pulls.

What I need, in my words:

class Game — one bowler's game, fed one ball at a time via Roll(int pins).
- Standard rules: 10 frames; strike = 10 + next two balls, spare = 10 + next one ball, open frame = pinfall. 10th frame: a strike earns two extra balls, a spare earns one, an open 10th ends the game after two.
- Validation is part of the contract: pinfall outside 0..10 -> ArgumentOutOfRangeException; a ball that would overfill the current rack (frame sum over 10 in frames 1-9, and the same rack logic inside the 10th — e.g. after a 10th-frame strike then a 5, a 7 is impossible on that rack) -> ArgumentException; any Roll after the game is complete -> InvalidOperationException.
- IsComplete property. Score() returns the final total but throws InvalidOperationException while the game is still going — mid-game running score comes from FrameScores().
- FrameScores() returns int?[10] of CUMULATIVE frame totals; a frame that can't be settled yet (strike/spare still waiting on bonus balls, or not yet bowled) is null.
- Scoreboard() returns exactly 10 lines joined by \n, one per frame: 'F{n} [{marks}] {cum}'. Marks are space-separated balls in the frame so far: 'X' for a strike, '/' for the ball that finishes off a rack for a spare, '-' for a gutter ball, otherwise the digit. The 10th frame just lists its two or three balls with the same symbols ('X X X', '2 / 6', 'X 9 /'). {cum} is the cumulative total, or '?' if the frame isn't settled or bowled; an unbowled frame is 'F7 [] ?'.
- static Game.Parse(string text): our roll-file format — whitespace-separated pinfall numbers, '#' starts a comment to end of line, blank lines fine. Non-numeric token -> FormatException; out-of-range number -> ArgumentOutOfRangeException (same as Roll). Parse feeds every roll into a fresh Game and returns it.

static class GameRunner — Run(string rollFileText) parses a COMPLETE game and returns the Scoreboard() plus a final line 'TOTAL {score}' (11 lines total). If the file doesn't finish the game, InvalidOperationException.

The fixture sheets cover a 300 game, an all-spares 150, a mixed sheet and a gutter game — the tests pin their scoreboards character for character, so follow the render spec exactly. Don't touch TenpinTests.cs, Tenpin.csproj, testdata/ or .gitignore.

```csharp
public static Game Parse(string text)
{
    var game = new Game();
    foreach (var rawLine in text.Split('\n'))
    {
        var line = rawLine;
        var hash = line.IndexOf('#');
        if (hash >= 0) line = line[..hash];
        foreach (var token in line.Split((char[]?)null, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries))
        {
            if (!int.TryParse(token, out var pins))
                throw new FormatException($"...");
            game.Roll(pins);
        }
    }
    return game;
}
```

```csharp
private FrameInfo[] GetFrames()
{
    var frames = new FrameInfo[10];
    int idx = 0;
    for (int f = 0; f < 10; f++)
    {
        int start = idx;
        int avail = _rolls.Count - idx;
        if (f < 9)
        {
            if (avail == 0) { frames[f] = new(start, 0, false, 0); }
            else if (_rolls[idx] == 10)
            {
                bool settled = avail >= 3;
                int score = settled ? 10 + _rolls[idx+1] + _rolls[idx+2] : 0;
                frames[f] = new(start, 1, settled, score);
                idx += 1;
            }
            else if (avail == 1) { frames[f] = new(start, 1, false, 0); idx += 1; }
            else
            {
                bool spare = _rolls[idx] + _rolls[idx+1] == 10;
                bool settled = !spare || avail >= 3;
                int score = settled ? (spare ? 10 + _rolls[idx+2] : _rolls[idx] + _rolls[idx+1]) : 0;
                frames[f] = new(start, 2, settled, score);
                idx += 2;
            }
        }
        else
        {
            frames[f] = new(start, avail, IsComplete, IsComplete ? SumFrom(idx) : 0);
            idx = _rolls.Count;
        }
    }
    return frames;
}
```

```csharp
}
```

```csharp
namespace Tenpin;

/// <summary>One bowler's game of ten-pin bowling, fed one ball at a time.</summary>
public class Game
{
    private const int FrameCount = 10;

    private readonly List<int> _rolls = new();
    private int _frame;          // 0-based index of the frame being bowled
    private int _frameBallCount; // balls bowled so far in the current frame
    private int _rack = 10;      // pins standing on the current rack
    private bool _tenthBonus;    // a third ball has been earned in the 10th frame

    public bool IsComplete { get; private set; }

    public void Roll(int pins)
    {
        if (IsComplete)
            throw new InvalidOperationException("The game is complete; no further rolls are allowed.");
        if (pins is < 0 or > 10)
            throw new ArgumentOutOfRangeException(nameof(pins), pins, "Pinfall must be between 0 and 10.");
        if (pins > _rack)
            throw new ArgumentException($"Only {_rack} pin(s) remain on the rack; {pins} cannot be scored.", nameof(pins));

        _rolls.Add(pins);
        _frameBallCount++;
        _rack -= pins;

        if (_frame < FrameCount - 1)
        {
            if (_rack == 0 || _frameBallCount == 2)
            {
                _frame++;
                _frameBallCount = 0;
                _rack = 10;
            }
        }
        else
        {
            if (_rack == 0)
            {
                _rack = 10;
                _tenthBonus = true;
            }
            if (_frameBallCount == 3 || (_frameBallCount == 2 && !_tenthBonus))
                IsComplete = true;
        }
    }

    public int Score()
    {
        if (!IsComplete)
            throw new InvalidOperationException("The game is still in progress; use FrameScores() for a running score.");
        return FrameScores()[FrameCount - 1]!.Value;
    }
    ...
}
```

RenderMarks:
```csharp
private static string RenderMarks(IReadOnlyList<int> balls)
{
    var marks = new List<string>(balls.Count);
    var freshRack = true;
    var previous = 0;
    foreach (var ball in balls)
    {
        if (freshRack)
        {
            marks.Add(ball == 10 ? "X" : ball == 0 ? "-" : ball.ToString());
            freshRack = ball == 10;
        }
        else
        {
            var cleared = previous + ball == 10;
            marks.Add(cleared ? "/" : ball == 0 ? "-" : ball.ToString());
            freshRack = cleared;
        }
        previous = ball;
    }
    return string.Join(' ', marks);
}
```

GetFrames:
```csharp
private FrameInfo[] GetFrames()
{
    var frames = new FrameInfo[FrameCount];
    var index = 0;
    for (var f = 0; f < FrameCount; f++)
    {
        var start = index;
        var available = _rolls.Count - index;
        if (f < FrameCount - 1)
        {
            if (available == 0)
            {
                frames[f] = new FrameInfo(start, 0, false, 0);
            }
            else if (_rolls[index] == 10)
            {
                var settled = available >= 3;
                frames[f] = new FrameInfo(start, 1, settled, settled ? 10 + _rolls[index + 1] + _rolls[index + 2] : 0);
                index += 1;
            }
            else if (available == 1)
            {
                frames[f] = new FrameInfo(start, 1, false, 0);
                index += 1;
            }
            else
            {
                var spare = _rolls[index] + _rolls[index + 1] == 10;
                var settled = !spare || available >= 3;
                var score = settled ? (spare ? 10 + _rolls[index + 2] : _rolls[index] + _rolls[index + 1]) : 0;
                frames[f] = new FrameInfo(start, 2, settled, score);
                index += 2;
            }
        }
        else
        {
            var total = 0;
            for (var i = index; i < _rolls.Count; i++) total += _rolls[i];
            frames[f] = new FrameInfo(start, available, IsComplete, IsComplete ? total : 0);
            index = _rolls.Count;
        }
    }
    return frames;
}
```

Parse:
```csharp
public static Game Parse(string text)
{
    var game = new Game();
    foreach (var rawLine in text.Split('\n'))
    {
        var line = rawLine;
        var comment = line.IndexOf('#');
        if (comment >= 0)
            line = line[..comment];
        foreach (var token in line.Split(' ', StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries))
        {
            if (!int.TryParse(token, NumberStyles.Integer, CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, out var pins))
                throw new FormatException($"Invalid pinfall token '{token}'.");
            game.Roll(pins);
        }
    }
    return game;
}
```

Hmm — `line.Split(' ', StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries)` splits only on space, not tabs! Tokens might be tab-separated ("whitespace-separated"). Test data uses spaces and newlines, but spec says whitespace. Since I split text on '\n' first, remaining whitespace could include '\r' (CRLF files) and '\t'. `line.Split(' ', RemoveEmptyEntries)` would leave "4\r" as token → TryParse with Integer style allows trailing white → parses fine. But tabs inside a line would break: "4\t5" → one token "4\t5" → TryParse fails (whitespace only allowed at ends) → FormatException incorrectly.

Better: split each line on all whitespace: `line.Split((char[]?)null, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries)` — passing null separators splits on whitespace. With nullable annotations, the signature is `Split(char[]? separator, StringSplitOptions options)`. Passing null literal is fine; might produce a warning? No — parameter is annotated nullable. Actually in .NET, `string.Split(char[]? separator, StringSplitOptions options)` — yes it's nullable-annotated. Good.

Alternatively use `line.Split(Array.Empty<char>(), StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries)` — empty array also means whitespace-split. Both fine. I'll use `(char[]?)null`.

messages.content:
=== MOONSHINER TASK BOUNDARY ===

Game night keeps ending in arguments about our house UNO variant, so I want a referee engine in C# that replays a hand from the deck we stacked and the moves we wrote down, and produces a transcript everyone can accept as final. Everything is deterministic: the deck is a fixture file, choices come from the script. Namespace Uno; the xunit suite UnoTests.cs is the contract (dotnet test); decks and a scripted hand live in testdata/. Nothing beyond what the csproj pulls.

Cards, as written on our score sheets: colors R G B Y. Number cards like 'R5' (digit 0-9). Action cards: 'GS' skip, 'GR' reverse, 'GD' draw-two. 'W' is the wild. Anything else in a deck file is a FormatException. Deck files: whitespace-separated tokens, '#' comments to end of line; first token is the top of the deck.

Game.Start(deckText, playerCount, handSize = 7): players are P1..Pn clockwise, playerCount 2-4 and handSize 1-10 (ArgumentException otherwise). Deal ONE card at a time round-robin starting with P1 until everyone has handSize, then flip the next card to start the discard. House law: our stacked decks always flip a NUMBER card — anything else is an ArgumentException, as is a deck too short to deal and flip. The flip sets the active color; P1 moves first; direction starts clockwise. The rest of the deck is the face-down pile, exposed as PileCount. Also expose CurrentPlayer, ActiveColor ('R'/'G'/'B'/'Y'), TopCard, DirectionClockwise, IsOver, Winner (null until someone wins), and Hand(player) — cards in the order acquired (ArgumentException for an unknown player).

Legality — static Rules.IsPlayable(card, top, activeColor): a wild is always playable; otherwise a card is playable if its color equals the active color, or it's a number card matching the top card's digit, or an action card matching the top card's action kind. (After a wild, the top card is 'W' — only the picked active color matters.)

Execute(command) plays ONE command for whoever's turn it is and returns the transcript lines it produced; RunScript(text) runs a whole move sheet ('#' comments, blank lines ignored) and returns all lines joined with \n. Commands:
- 'play <card>' / 'play W <color>': the current player must hold the card (InvalidOperationException), it must be playable (InvalidOperationException), a wild must come with a color and a non-wild must not (InvalidOperationException either way; a malformed card token or unknown command word is FormatException). Line: 'P1 plays R5', or 'P1 plays W and picks B'. The played card becomes the top card and its color (or the picked color) becomes active.
- 'draw' / 'draw <color>': the current player takes the top pile card. FORCED-DRAW-THEN-PLAY, our core house rule: if the drawn card is playable it is played immediately — line 'P3 draws G7 and plays it', with wilds 'P1 draws W and plays it and picks R' taking the scripted color (missing color for a drawn wild, or a color supplied when the drawn card is not a wild: InvalidOperationException, and the rejected command must leave the pile and hands untouched so the sheet can be corrected and retried). If it is not playable the player keeps it and the turn passes: 'P2 draws B2 and keeps it'. Drawing from an empty pile is an InvalidOperationException.

Effects after a play: skip — the next player in the current direction loses their turn, line '{P} is skipped'. Reverse — direction flips, line 'direction is now counter-clockwise' (or 'clockwise'); with exactly TWO players it additionally acts as a skip (both lines, actor goes again). Draw-two — the next player takes two cards off the pile and loses their turn, single line '{P} draws 2 and is skipped'. Number cards and wilds have no effect line. If a play empties the player's hand the game ends IMMEDIATELY: the win line '{P} wins' replaces any effect lines, IsOver flips, Winner is set, and any further command is an InvalidOperationException.

The suite diffs a full scripted three-hander transcript character for character and pins hands, pile counts, turn order and every error path, so follow the wording and the laws exactly. Don't touch UnoTests.cs, Uno.csproj, testdata/ or .gitignore.


PRIOR ATTEMPT FEEDBACK (address before finishing):
- missing_requirements: Playable action cards drawn by the forced-draw rule are not given their skip, reverse, or draw-two effects.
- bugs_and_regressions: Game.Draw directly advances to the next player after force-playing any non-wild card. For a drawn playable skip, reverse, or draw-two, it omits required effect transcript lines and produces incorrect direction, hands, pile count, and turn order.
- requirement unmet: Implement atomic forced-draw-then-play behavior
- requirement unmet: Apply skip, reverse, and draw-two effects after every play

```csharp
namespace Uno;

internal enum CardKind { Number, Skip, Reverse, DrawTwo, Wild }

internal readonly struct Card
{
    public string Text { get; }
    public char Color { get; }   // '\0' for wild
    public CardKind Kind { get; }
    public int Digit { get; }    // -1 unless Number

    private Card(string text, char color, CardKind kind, int digit)
    {
        Text = text; Color = color; Kind = kind; Digit = digit;
    }

    public bool IsWild => Kind == CardKind.Wild;

    public static Card Parse(string token)
    {
        if (token == "W")
            return new Card("W", '\0', CardKind.Wild, -1);
        if (token.Length == 2 && IsColor(token[0]))
        {
            char c = token[1];
            if (c >= '0' && c <= '9')
                return new Card(token, token[0], CardKind.Number, c - '0');
            return c switch
            {
                'S' => new Card(token, token[0], CardKind.Skip, -1),
                'R' => new Card(token, token[0], CardKind.Reverse, -1),
                'D' => new Card(token, token[0], CardKind.DrawTwo, -1),
                _ => throw new FormatException($"malformed card token: '{token}'"),
            };
        }
        throw new FormatException($"malformed card token: '{token}'");
    }

    public static bool IsColor(char c) => c is 'R' or 'G' or 'B' or 'Y';
}
```

Rules:

```csharp
public static class Rules
{
    public static bool IsPlayable(string card, string top, string activeColor) =>
        IsPlayable(Card.Parse(card), Card.Parse(top), activeColor);

    internal static bool IsPlayable(Card card, Card top, string activeColor)
    {
        if (card.IsWild) return true;
        if (activeColor.Length == 1 && card.Color == activeColor[0]) return true;
        if (card.Kind == CardKind.Number && top.Kind == CardKind.Number && card.Digit == top.Digit) return true;
        if (card.Kind is CardKind.Skip or CardKind.Reverse or CardKind.DrawTwo && card.Kind == top.Kind) return true;
        return false;
    }
}
```

```csharp
public sealed class Game
{
    private readonly List<Card> _pile;      // face-down pile, index 0 = top
    private readonly List<List<Card>> _hands;
    private readonly int _playerCount;
    private int _current;                   // 0-based
    private bool _clockwise = true;
    private Card _top;
    private char _activeColor;

    public string CurrentPlayer => PlayerName(_current);
    public string ActiveColor => _activeColor.ToString();
    public string TopCard => _top.Text;
    public bool DirectionClockwise => _clockwise;
    public int PileCount => _pile.Count;
    public bool IsOver { get; private set; }
    public string? Winner { get; private set; }

    private Game(...) {}

    public static Game Start(string deckText, int playerCount, int handSize = 7)
    {
        if (playerCount < 2 || playerCount > 4)
            throw new ArgumentException("playerCount must be 2-4");
        if (handSize < 1 || handSize > 10)
            throw new ArgumentException("handSize must be 1-10");

        var deck = ParseDeck(deckText);
        if (deck.Count < playerCount * handSize + 1)
            throw new ArgumentException("deck too small to deal and flip");

        var game = new Game(playerCount);
        // deal round-robin
        int pos = 0;
        for (int round = 0; round < handSize; round++)
            for (int p = 0; p < playerCount; p++)
                game._hands[p].Add(deck[pos++]);

        var flip = deck[pos++];
        if (flip.Kind != CardKind.Number)
            throw new ArgumentException("the flipped starter must be a number card");
        game._top = flip;
        game._activeColor = flip.Color;
        for (; pos < deck.Count; pos++)
            game._pile.Add(deck[pos]);
        return game;
    }
    ...
}
```

```csharp
private static List<Card> ParseDeck(string deckText)
{
    var cards = new List<Card>();
    foreach (var rawLine in deckText.Split('\n'))
    {
        var line = rawLine;
        int hash = line.IndexOf('#');
        if (hash >= 0) line = line[..hash];
        foreach (var token in line.Split((char[]?)null, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries))
            cards.Add(Card.Parse(token));
    }
    return cards;
}
```

`line.Split((char[]?)null, ...)` — with TreatWarningsAsErrors and nullable, `Split(null, ...)` ambiguity? `string.Split(char[]? separator, StringSplitOptions options)` exists. Passing `(char[]?)null` is fine. Or use `line.Split(' ', StringSplitOptions...)` but that won't split tabs. Whitespace-separated → use the null-separator overload which splits on all whitespace. In modern .NET, `line.Split(null, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries)` may produce a nullable warning; casting to `(char[]?)null` resolves. Alternatively `line.Split((char[])[" "])`... no. I'll use the cast. Actually simpler: `foreach (var token in line.Split(' ', '\t', '\r'))`... hmm overload `Split(params char[])` doesn't take options → empty entries possible with multiple spaces; filter manually. Cleanest: `line.Split((char[]?)null, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries | StringSplitOptions.TrimEntries)`.

```csharp
public string[] Execute(string command)
{
    if (IsOver) throw new InvalidOperationException("the game is over");
    var tokens = Tokenize(command);
    if (tokens.Count == 0) throw new FormatException("empty command");
    return tokens[0] switch
    {
        "play" => Play(tokens),
        "draw" => Draw(tokens),
        _ => throw new FormatException($"unknown command: '{tokens[0]}'"),
    };
}
```

Play:

```csharp
private string[] Play(IReadOnlyList<string> tokens)
{
    if (tokens.Count is < 2 or > 3)
        throw new FormatException("'play' expects a card and, for a wild, a color");
    var card = Card.Parse(tokens[1]);
    char? pick = null;
    if (tokens.Count == 3)
    {
        if (!card.IsWild)
            throw new InvalidOperationException("only a wild takes a color");
        pick = ParseColor(tokens[2]);
    }
    else if (card.IsWild)
        throw new InvalidOperationException("a wild must be played with a color");

    var hand = _hands[_current];
    int at = hand.FindIndex(c => c.Text == card.Text);
    if (at < 0)
        throw new InvalidOperationException($"{CurrentPlayer} does not hold {card.Text}");
    if (!Rules.IsPlayable(card, _top, ActiveColor))
        throw new InvalidOperationException($"{card.Text} is not playable");

    hand.RemoveAt(at);
    var lines = new List<string> { pick is char c ? $"{CurrentPlayer} plays W and picks {c}" : $"{CurrentPlayer} plays {card.Text}" };
    ResolvePlay(card, pick, lines);
    return lines.ToArray();
}
```

ResolvePlay — shared by play and forced draw:

```csharp
private void ResolvePlay(Card card, char? pick, List<string> lines)
{
    _top = card;
    _activeColor = pick ?? card.Color;
    if (_hands[_current].Count == 0)
    {
        lines.Add($"{CurrentPlayer} wins");
        IsOver = true;
        Winner = CurrentPlayer;
        return;
    }
    switch (card.Kind)
    {
        case CardKind.Skip:
        {
            int victim = Next(_current);
            lines.Add($"{PlayerName(victim)} is skipped");
            _current = Next(victim);
            break;
        }
        case CardKind.Reverse:
        {
            _clockwise = !_clockwise;
            lines.Add(_clockwise ? "direction is now clockwise" : "direction is now counter-clockwise");
            if (_playerCount == 2)
            {
                int victim = Next(_current);
                lines.Add($"{PlayerName(victim)} is skipped");
                _current = Next(victim);
            }
            else
                _current = Next(_current);
            break;
        }
        case CardKind.DrawTwo:
        {
            int victim = Next(_current);
            for (int i = 0; i < 2 && _pile.Count > 0; i++)
            {
                _hands[victim].Add(_pile[0]);
                _pile.RemoveAt(0);
            }
            lines.Add($"{PlayerName(victim)} draws 2 and is skipped");
            _current = Next(victim);
            break;
        }
        default:
            _current = Next(_current);
            break;
    }
}
```

```csharp
private string[] Draw(IReadOnlyList<string> tokens)
{
    if (tokens.Count > 2)
        throw new FormatException("'draw' expects at most a color");
    char? pick = null;
    if (tokens.Count == 2)
        pick = ParseColor(tokens[1]);
    if (_pile.Count == 0)
        throw new InvalidOperationException("the pile is empty");

    var drawn = _pile[0];  // peek; nothing is consumed until the command is validated
    if (drawn.IsWild && pick is null)
        throw new InvalidOperationException("a drawn wild must be played with a color");
    if (!drawn.IsWild && pick is not null)
        throw new InvalidOperationException("only a drawn wild takes a color");

    _pile.RemoveAt(0);
    var lines = new List<string>();
    if (Rules.IsPlayable(drawn, _top, ActiveColor))
    {
        lines.Add(pick is char c
            ? $"{CurrentPlayer} draws W and plays it and picks {c}"
            : $"{CurrentPlayer} draws {drawn.Text} and plays it");
        ResolvePlay(drawn, pick, lines);
    }
    else
    {
        _hands[_current].Add(drawn);
        lines.Add($"{CurrentPlayer} draws {drawn.Text} and keeps it");
        _current = Next(_current);
    }
    return lines.ToArray();
}
```

=== MOONSHINER TASK BOUNDARY ===

Our office chat runs a daily Wordle-style puzzle against a curated candidate pool, and I want a SOLVER library I can script against it — not the game itself, just the deduction side. C#, namespace WordleSolver, xunit suite in WordleSolverTests.cs is the acceptance contract (dotnet test), fixture pool in testdata/candidates.txt. No packages beyond the csproj.

Two pieces:

static class Feedbacks — Score(string guess, string answer) returns the 5-char feedback string the puzzle would show, 'G'/'Y'/'B' per position. The duplicate-letter law, exactly: greens are decided first and consume their answer letter; then the remaining guess letters are scanned left to right, and each one earns a 'Y' only if an unconsumed copy of that letter is still available in the answer (consuming it), otherwise 'B'. Both words must be exactly five lowercase ASCII letters or you throw ArgumentException.

class Solver — constructed from the candidate-list text plus a pinned opening guess:
- List format: one word per line, '#' starts a comment to end of line, blank lines ignored. Every word must be five lowercase ASCII letters (FormatException otherwise); duplicates are an ArgumentException. The opener must be five lowercase letters too (ArgumentException), but does NOT have to be in the list.
- Candidates: the remaining pool, always exposed in ascending ordinal order. Remaining: its count.
- NextGuess(): before any feedback it's the pinned opener, verbatim. After that, the policy is lexicographic-first: the smallest (ordinal) word still in the pool. Empty pool -> InvalidOperationException.
- Observe(guess, feedback): keep exactly the words w for which Score(guess, w) equals the feedback. Feedback must be five chars of G/Y/B (ArgumentException). Observing something contradictory can legitimately empty the pool.
- Solve(answer, maxGuesses = 6): plays the solver against a known answer — opener first, then lexicographic-first after each feedback — and returns the guess sequence ending with the answer. It must work from a FRESH copy of the full pool and leave the solver's interactive state untouched. The answer must be in the candidate list (ArgumentException); running past maxGuesses is an InvalidOperationException.

The tests pin exact candidate counts and pool contents after specific feedback, and exact guess sequences to several answers, so the filtering and the tie-break policy have to be to the letter. Don't touch WordleSolverTests.cs, WordleSolver.csproj, testdata/ or .gitignore.

messages.content:
=== MOONSHINER TASK BOUNDARY ===

Our community newsletter prints a word-search puzzle every issue, and I'm sick of building the grids in a spreadsheet. I want a C# library with two halves: a GENERATOR that lays out the same grid for the same seed every time (the print shop reprints old issues, so reproducibility is non-negotiable), and a SOLVER our proofreader runs to list every placement in a finished grid. Namespace WordSearch; the xunit suite WordSearchTests.cs is the contract (dotnet test); reference grids in testdata/. No packages beyond the csproj.

Determinism comes from this exact generator recipe:

class Lcg — Lcg(int seed), seed must be non-negative (ArgumentException). Next() advances state = (state * 1103515245 + 12345) mod 2^31 and returns the new state (do the multiply in 64-bit). Rand(int k) = Next() % k, k must be positive (ArgumentOutOfRangeException).

static Generator.Generate(int height, int width, IReadOnlyList<string> words, int seed) -> the grid as height lines joined by \n, no separators. Words are uppercased on intake; after uppercasing each must be 2+ chars of A-Z only (ArgumentException) and duplicates are an ArgumentException. One Lcg seeded once drives the whole build. Words are placed IN THE GIVEN ORDER; for each word, up to 100 attempts: draw d = Rand(8), then r = Rand(height), then c = Rand(width) — in that draw order, every attempt draws all three. Direction index d maps to the pinned compass order E, SE, S, SW, W, NW, N, NE — E is (row+0, col+1), SE is (+1,+1), S is (+1,0), SW is (+1,-1), W is (0,-1), NW is (-1,-1), N is (-1,0), NE is (-1,+1). The attempt succeeds if the whole word stays in bounds starting at (r, c) heading d, and every cell is either still empty or ALREADY HOLDS THE SAME LETTER — overlap is allowed and intended, that's what makes the puzzles good. On success the word is written; after 100 failed attempts Generate throws InvalidOperationException naming the word. After all words, fill the empty cells in row-major order (left-to-right, top-to-bottom), each cell getting (char)('A' + Rand(26)).

static Solver.Find(string gridText, IReadOnlyList<string> words) -> IReadOnlyList<Placement>. The grid must be non-empty rows of equal length, A-Z only (FormatException). Query words get the same normalization/validation as the generator. Find EVERY placement of every query word: all starting cells, all 8 directions (a palindrome legitimately shows up once per direction). Sort the results by word (ordinal), then row, then column, then direction in the pinned compass order above. Placement exposes Word, Row, Col, Direction (the compass string), and ToString() is '{Word} {Row} {Col} {Direction}'. Words that don't occur just don't appear.

The tests pin whole grids for given seeds and complete found-lists, so the draw order, compass order, overlap rule and fill order all have to be exact. Don't touch WordSearchTests.cs, WordSearch.csproj, testdata/ or .gitignore.

messages.content:
=== MOONSHINER TASK BOUNDARY ===

Ground-ops software, baggage hall. Between check-in and the tarmac there's a makeup carousel: check-in agents put bags on, ONE loader takes them off and stacks carts. The current code models this with a lock, a List, and a Thread.Sleep polling loop, and it either spins the CPU or loses the last bags of a flight when the belt closes. Rebuild it on System.Threading.Channels. C# / .NET 10, namespace BagLine, single project. CarouselTests.cs is the acceptance suite — hands off it, the csproj, and .gitignore.

Model: sealed record Bag(string Tag, string Flight). The belt is class MakeupCarousel, backed by a BOUNDED Channel<Bag>:
- ctor MakeupCarousel(int capacity) — physical belt length; capacity < 1 is ArgumentOutOfRangeException.
- Task PlaceAsync(Bag bag, CancellationToken ct = default) — puts a bag on the belt. With a free slot it completes immediately; on a full belt it WAITS for a slot (that's the whole point — backpressure, not bag piles on the floor). If the agent gives up (token fires) the wait ends canceled and that bag must never appear on the belt. Placing onto a closed belt surfaces the channel's own ChannelClosedException — don't wrap it.
- bool TryPlace(Bag bag) — non-waiting variant for the overflow desk: true when the bag went on, false when the belt is full or closed.
- void CloseFlight() — no more bags for this flight; calling it again is a no-op. Bags already on the belt still get loaded.
- Task<IReadOnlyList<string>> LoadAsync(Func<Bag, Task<string>> loader) — the single loader: takes bags off STRICTLY in placement order, runs the loader callback for one bag AT A TIME (never overlapping — one person, two hands), collects the callback results in that same order, and finishes when the flight is closed and the belt is empty.

Hard rule from our platform team, non-negotiable: no Task.Delay, no Thread.Sleep, no timers, no polling loops anywhere in the implementation — the channel's own async coordination does all the waiting. The tests script the loader with TaskCompletionSource gates and will catch both busy-waiting shortcuts and overlapping loads. `dotnet test` green = done.

Key design decisions:
- `MakeupCarousel(int capacity)`: explicit `capacity < 1` check → `ArgumentOutOfRangeException` (cleaner param name than the channel's internal one).
- Bounded channel, `FullMode = Wait` (default), `SingleReader = true` (one loader), writers from many agents.
- `PlaceAsync` → `Writer.WriteAsync(bag, ct).AsTask()` — needs `Task` return type for `Assert.ThrowsAsync<ChannelClosedException>`; cancellation handled by channel; closed-channel surfaces `ChannelClosedException` unwrapped.
- `TryPlace` → `Writer.TryWrite`.
- `CloseFlight` → `Writer.TryComplete()` (idempotent).
- `LoadAsync` → `await foreach (ReadAllAsync())`, sequential `await loader(bag)`, collect into `List<string>`. No delays, no polling.

**Root cause / starting state:** the baseline commit contained only the acceptance suite, csproj, and .gitignore — no working implementation at all. The old lock+List+`Thread.Sleep` design's failure modes (CPU spin, lost bags at close) are exactly what a bounded channel eliminates structurally.

**What I changed:** added one file, `MakeupCarousel.cs` (namespace `BagLine`), containing:
- `public sealed record Bag(string Tag, string Flight)`
- `MakeupCarousel` backed by `Channel.CreateBounded<Bag>(capacity)` with `FullMode.Wait`, `SingleReader = true` (one loader), `SingleWriter = false` (many agents):
  - **ctor** — explicit `capacity < 1` → `ArgumentOutOfRangeException` before touching the channel.
  - **PlaceAsync** — `Writer.WriteAsync(bag, ct).AsTask()`. Free slot → completes synchronously; full belt → the channel suspends the write (backpressure); fired token → canceled `ValueTask` and the channel guarantees the bag is never enqueued; closed belt → the channel's own `ChannelClosedException` propagates unwrapped.
  - **TryPlace** — `Writer.TryWrite`, honest false on full *or* closed.
  - **CloseFlight** — `Writer.TryComplete()`, naturally idempotent; queued bags still drain.
  - **LoadAsync** — `await foreach` over `Reader.ReadAllAsync()`, invoking the loader callback with a plain sequential `await` per bag, so bags come off strictly in placement order, never overlap, results collect in order, and the enumeration ends exactly when the flight is closed and the belt is empty.

**Proof:**
- `dotnet test` → `Passed! - Failed: 0, Passed: 8, Total: 8` (run twice, stable).
- `grep` over the implementation finds no `Task.Delay`, `Thread.Sleep`, timers, or polling loops — all waiting is the channel's own async coordination (`WriteAsync` + `ReadAllAsync`).

- Direct aggregate callers

And because the handler throws before `SaveAsync`/`PublishAsync`, no side effects; because the mediator only calls `_idempotency.Store` after the handler succeeds, a failed transition never poisons the cache. messages.content:
Fixed. All 11 tests pass with a single four-line guard in the aggregate.

**Root cause:** `Claim.Submit()` in `DomainClaim.cs` performed the transition unconditionally. A closed (or draft, or already-submitted) claim sent through `ClaimsController` → mediator → `SubmitClaimHandler` had its status, submitter, timestamp, and version blindly overwritten, and the handler then saved and published a second `ClaimSubmittedEvent`. The `InvalidClaimTransitionException` type existed but was never thrown anywhere.

**The fix (the seam safe for every caller):** a guard at the top of `Claim.Submit()` — the domain aggregate itself, which every path funnels through:

```csharp
if (Status != ClaimStatus.ReadyForSubmission)
    throw new InvalidClaimTransitionException(
        $"claim {Id} cannot transition from {Status} to Submitted");
```

Why this seam, and why nothing else needed changing:
- **Aggregate-level enforcement** protects the controller/mediator path, direct handler callers, and direct aggregate callers identically — no behavior is duplicated upstream.
- The guard throws **before any mutation**, so status, version, submitter, and timestamp stay intact; and since it precedes `checked(Version + 1)`, the version-overflow path still throws `OverflowException` on a `ReadyForSubmission` claim with all fields untouched.
- The handler only calls `SaveAsync`/`PublishAsync` after `Submit` returns, so a rejected transition produces **no save and no event**.
- The mediator only calls `_idempotency.Store` after the handler **succeeds**, so a 409 never poisons the idempotency key — a later retry with the same key executes fresh.
- The controller already mapped `InvalidClaimTransitionException` → 409 with the exception message as the body.

**Verification:** `dotnet test` — 11/11 passed, including the incident regression (`ClosedClaimCannotBeResubmittedAndRemainsUntouched`), direct-handler protection, idempotency non-poisoning/replay/conflict, unauthorized-stops-before-store, validation ordering, and checked-overflow purity. Only `DomainClaim.cs` was modified; the test file, project file, and ignore file are untouched.

=== MOONSHINER TASK BOUNDARY ===

I write the ops software for a three-berth ferry terminal. Right now every department polls everyone else's database tables to find out what changed, and last week the departure board showed a sailing at berth 3 for twenty minutes after dispatch had moved it. I want a small in-process event hub the modules can share — typed events, plain delegates, no reflection magic, no external bus. C# / .NET 10, namespace FerryHub, single project.

Event payloads are records I want declared exactly like this (the modules already agreed on them): BerthAssigned(string Sailing, int Berth), SailingDelayed(string Sailing, int Minutes), RampFault(int Ramp, string Note).

The hub itself is class OpsHub:
- Subscription Subscribe<TEvent>(Action<TEvent> handler) — registers a handler for exactly TEvent and hands back an opaque Subscription token. Null handler is an ArgumentNullException at subscribe time, not a surprise at publish time. Subscribing the same delegate twice is two registrations with two independent tokens.
- bool Unsubscribe(Subscription subscription) — true when it removed the registration, false when that token was already gone (callers retry on reconnect and I don't want that throwing).
- PublishResult Publish<TEvent>(TEvent evt) — invokes the TEvent handlers in the order they subscribed. sealed record PublishResult(int Delivered, IReadOnlyList<Exception> Errors): Delivered counts handlers that completed, Errors collects what the rest threw, in handler order. The iron rule from the berth-3 incident: one crashing handler must NEVER stop the handlers behind it — catch, collect, keep going. Publishing a type nobody listens to is fine (0 delivered, no errors).
- Dispatch runs against a snapshot of the registration list taken the moment Publish starts — same rule the BCL uses for multicast delegates. A handler that unsubscribes anyone (itself included) or subscribes someone new changes the NEXT publish, never the one in flight.
- public event Action<string, int>? Dispatched — a plain C# event the audit console taps with += / -=. After the handlers have run (even when some of them threw), raise it once with the event type's short name and the delivered count. It fires for subscriber-less publishes too.

Everything is single-threaded in our processes, so no locking gymnastics needed — correctness of ordering and isolation is what I care about. OpsHubTests.cs is the acceptance contract; don't touch it, the csproj, or .gitignore. `dotnet test` green means done.

messages.content:
Done — `dotnet test` is green: **13/13 passed, 0 warnings, 0 errors.** Only two new files were added; `OpsHubTests.cs`, the csproj, and `.gitignore` are untouched.

**What I built**

- **`Events.cs`** — the three agreed payloads, declared exactly as specified: `BerthAssigned(string Sailing, int Berth)`, `SailingDelayed(string Sailing, int Minutes)`, `RampFault(int Ramp, string Note)`.
- **`OpsHub.cs`** — `OpsHub`, the opaque `Subscription` token, and `sealed record PublishResult(int Delivered, IReadOnlyList<Exception> Errors)`.

**How the hub meets each requirement**

- **Typed, delegate-based, no reflection** — registrations are stored in a `List<Subscription>` where each token carries its `Type` and the `Action<TEvent>` as a `Delegate`; `Publish<TEvent>` filters by exact type match and casts back, which is safe because the type was bound at subscribe time.
- **Subscribe** — `ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(handler)` rejects nulls up front; every call appends a new token, so the same delegate subscribed twice yields two independent registrations (removal is by token reference identity).
- **Unsubscribe** — plain `List.Remove`, returning `true`/`false`; a stale token from a reconnect retry just returns `false`, never throws.
- **Exception isolation** — each handler invocation is wrapped in try/catch: successes increment `Delivered`, thrown exceptions are appended to `Errors` in handler order, and the loop always continues (the berth-3 rule).
- **Snapshot semantics** — `Publish` copies the registration list with `ToArray()` before invoking anything, so mid-dispatch subscribes/unsubscribes (self included) only affect the *next* publish, matching multicast-delegate behavior.
- **`Dispatched` tap** — a plain `event Action<string, int>?` raised once after the handler loop with `typeof(TEvent).Name` and the delivered count, regardless of handler errors or zero subscribers; `+=`/`−=` get standard C# event ordering and removal.

No locking anywhere, per your single-threaded constraint.
